questions.question: Who is allowed to connect SharePoint and OneDrive to Notion AI?

options.text: Any Notion user
options.explanation: All Notion users cannot connect; the integration requires specific admin rights.

options.text: A Microsoft 365 Admin who also owns the Notion workspace and is on a Business or Enterprise plan
options.explanation: Correct – both Microsoft 365 admin status and workspace ownership on a Business/Enterprise plan are required.

options.text: Only developers with API access
options.explanation: Developers alone do not meet the admin and ownership requirements.

options.text: Anyone with a personal OneDrive account
options.explanation: Personal OneDrive accounts are not supported for this connector.

questions.question: Which file formats can Notion AI read from SharePoint and OneDrive?

options.text: DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and PDF
options.explanation: These are the supported document types listed in the article.

options.text: HTML web pages
options.explanation: SharePoint site pages (HTML) are explicitly not readable.

options.text: MP3 audio files
options.explanation: Audio formats are not mentioned as supported.

options.text: All file types
options.explanation: Only the four listed types are supported; others are not.

questions.question: How long can the initial connection process take to complete?

options.text: Up to 12 hours
options.explanation: The article states a longer maximum duration.

options.text: Up to 24 hours
options.explanation: While possible, the stated upper bound is higher.

options.text: Up to 36 hours
options.explanation: The connector can take as much as 36 hours to finish syncing.

options.text: Immediately after clicking Done
options.explanation: Syncing is asynchronous and may take many hours.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a supported use case for the SharePoint connector?

options.text: Administrative questions about company policies
options.explanation: Administrative questions are explicitly listed as a use case.

options.text: Project management queries involving team files
options.explanation: Project management queries are supported.

options.text: Reading SharePoint site pages
options.explanation: The connector cannot read SharePoint site pages.

options.text: Document analysis for insights
options.explanation: Document analysis is a core capability of the connector.

questions.question: How does Notion AI enforce Microsoft permissions when accessing SharePoint/OneDrive files?

options.text: It ignores Microsoft permissions and shows all files to every user
options.explanation: Notion AI strictly follows existing Microsoft permissions.

options.text: It maps Microsoft members to their Notion accounts and respects ACLs via the Microsoft Graph API
options.explanation: Correct – Notion syncs permissions using Graph API endpoints and enforces them.

options.text: It grants full access to all users after the initial sync
options.explanation: Access is continuously checked; no blanket full access is given.

options.text: It checks permissions only once during setup
options.explanation: Permissions are polled and updated hourly, not just once.

questions.question: If a user is a Notion workspace owner but not a Microsoft 365 Admin, can they connect the SharePoint & OneDrive connector?

options.text: Yes, workspace ownership is sufficient
options.explanation: Both admin rights and ownership are required.

options.text: No, they must also be a Microsoft 365 Admin
options.explanation: The article specifies that a Microsoft 365 Admin is mandatory.

options.text: Only if they are on a Free Notion plan
options.explanation: Plan level is not the limiting factor; admin status is.

options.text: Only if they have a personal OneDrive linked
options.explanation: Personal OneDrives are not supported.

questions.question: What happens to SharePoint and OneDrive data after a workspace disconnects the connector?

options.text: The data remains searchable indefinitely
options.explanation: Data becomes unsearchable immediately after disconnection.

options.text: The data is immediately unsearchable and deleted within 24 hours
options.explanation: Correct – Notion follows data minimization principles and deletes within 24 hours.

options.text: The data is transferred to a backup archive for compliance
options.explanation: No backup archive is mentioned; the data is deleted.

options.text: The data is archived for future re‑connection
options.explanation: Archiving is not part of the described process.

questions.question: How frequently does Notion sync permission changes from Microsoft?

options.text: Every minute
options.explanation: The sync interval is longer than a minute.

options.text: Every hour
options.explanation: The article states that permission updates are synced hourly.

options.text: Every day
options.explanation: Daily sync would be too slow for the described behavior.

options.text: Weekly
options.explanation: Weekly sync would not meet the need for near‑real‑time permission enforcement.

questions.question: Which statement best describes how Notion stores SharePoint/OneDrive content for AI queries?

options.text: It stores raw files on its servers for direct access
options.explanation: Notion stores embeddings, not raw files.

options.text: It stores embeddings in a vector database like Turbopuffer, which has SOC2 Type II certification
options.explanation: Correct – embeddings in a vetted vector DB enable efficient search and meet security standards.

options.text: It stores only metadata about each file
options.explanation: Metadata alone would not allow content‑based queries.

options.text: It stores encrypted copies of PDFs only
options.explanation: Notion handles multiple file types and uses embeddings, not just PDFs.

questions.question: A user wants to limit AI searches to only SharePoint and OneDrive content. Which steps should they take?

options.text: In Notion AI click “Find in” and select “SharePoint and OneDrive”
options.explanation: This is the correct UI action to restrict the search scope.

options.text: Change their Microsoft admin settings to restrict access
options.explanation: Admin settings affect permissions, not the AI search scope within Notion.

options.text: Disable Notion AI globally for the workspace
options.explanation: Disabling AI would prevent all searches, not just limit the scope.

options.text: Use a custom API endpoint to filter results
options.explanation: The article describes a built‑in UI toggle, not a custom API solution.
